content.text:
Identifying two issues: `hideEmpty` isn't on by default (and "Clear all" turns it off), and "Newest first" sort ignores `newest_job_fetched`. Applying fixes.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string: <input type="checkbox" id="hideEmpty" class="filter-toggle-input" />
input.new_string: <input type="checkbox" id="hideEmpty" class="filter-toggle-input" checked />

input.description: Run unit tests that may not need full flask

content.text:
Two separate issues were at play:

### 1. “Hide companies with no open roles” (`hideEmpty`)
The filter was **off by default**, so the board loaded with `hide_empty=0` and showed companies with no open roles. Toggling it could also feel broken if you hit **Clear all**, which turned `hideEmpty` off along with everything else.

**Fix:**
- Default the checkbox to **checked** in `index.html`
- In `storage.js`, treat missing saved preference as **on** (so existing users without a saved value get the new default)
- **Clear all** now resets `hideEmpty` back to **on** (baseline), while users can still uncheck it manually to see empty companies

### 2. “Newest first” sort
Sort used `latest_fetched` / `updated` but ignored `newest_job_fetched`, which the server sets from the latest fetch activity. That made “Newest first” order wrong after fetches.

**Fix:** `companyActivityTs()` in `render.js` now prefers `newest_job_fetched` first (same as the backend stats logic).

---

After a refresh, the panel should default to companies-with-jobs-only, and newest-first sorting should track recent fetch activity correctly. Uncheck **Hide companies with no open roles** in Filters if you want to see companies with no open roles.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
waiting referal has issue and not showing it goes hidden when i click on and cannot do anything
</user_query>

Investigating how "waiting referral" is handled in the UI and board update logic.

[REDACTED]

The referral popover stays inside the React tree while ATS/hide-reason popovers mount to `document.body`, so React re-renders reset `hidden` and break interaction. Applying the same floating-mount pattern.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string: <script type="module" src="/static/js/main.js?v=96"></script>
input.old_string: <script type="module" src="/static/js/main.js?v=95"></script>

content.text:
The referral popover was broken for the same reason ATS and hide-reason popovers were fixed earlier: it lived inside the React tree with `hidden` always set in JSX, while vanilla JS toggled `hidden` on click. Any board re-render immediately closed it again — so it looked like nothing happened.

**Fix:** use the same floating-mount pattern as the other popovers:
- Move the referral popover to `document.body` when opened (outside React’s control)
- Resolve the parent wrap from popover buttons via `data-referral-wrap-id`
- Unmount and clean up orphaned popovers on close

After a hard refresh, clicking **Waiting referral** should open the LinkedIn dialog and stay open until you save, clear, or dismiss it. The active state (badge + highlighted button) should persist after saving.

[REDACTED]

Tracing the full referral click flow and comparing it with the working popover implementations.

[REDACTED]

The referral popover is mounted outside `#jobs`, but click handlers still live on `#jobs` — so Save/Close never fire. Moving referral UI to a React-controlled portal like the other widgets.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
The `#jobs` click handler was closing the referral popover on the same click that opened it. Skipping referral elements in that handler.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string: <script type="module" src="/static/js/main.js?v=98"></script>
input.old_string: <script type="module" src="/static/js/main.js?v=97"></script>

content.text:
The real bug was a race between React and the old vanilla click handler.

**What was happening**
1. React opened the referral dialog on button click.
2. The same click bubbled to the `#jobs` listener, which immediately called `closeReferralPopovers()`.
3. The dialog flashed closed — it looked like nothing worked.

**What we changed**
1. **ReferralWidget is now fully React-controlled** — open/close state, portal to `document.body`, save/clear wired to the API.
2. **Skipped referral elements** in the `#jobs` “click outside to close” handler so opening doesn’t instantly close.
3. **Rebuilt** the React bundle (`board.js`).

Do a **hard refresh** (Cmd+Shift+R) so you load `main.js?v=98` and the new `board.js`.

Then click **Waiting referral** — the LinkedIn dialog should stay open, let you paste a URL, and save. If it still fails, tell me what you see (any toast error, console message, etc.).

[REDACTED]
